item.title: VIDEO: Orsted installs first monopile at Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm
item.description: Watch the first of 197 XXL monopiles being installed on Orsted’s Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm in the UK North Sea. The XXL monopiles are the largest used by Orsted on any of its European wind farms to date and each will be topped by a 15MW turbine. They were installed using Cadeler’s Wind Ally installation vessel. (Video credit: Orsted)

item.title: Chinese wind turbine makers sweep global top five for first time
item.description: China-based OEMs even more dominant at top of rankings thanks to huge domestic onshore wind market and increasing ambitions abroad, says Global Wind Energy Council

item.title: CIP leads the pack as Korea's latest offshore wind auction attracts bumper capacity
item.description: Korean renewables tender could provide some interesting answers on the future of wind turbine manufacture and assembly in the region

item.title: Deals of the Week: Envision, Nexans, Iberdrola, Mubadala
item.description: China's globetrotting wind and storage giant opens a new market as a clutch of big names in renewables engage in M&A agreements

item.title: Strong policy, weak delivery: Statkraft boss on where UK and Ireland fall short on renewables
item.description: Statkraft is building across wind, solar, batteries and grid-stability projects in the UK and Ireland, where rising renewables penetration is forcing faster progress on storage, hybrid projects and system integration

item.title: Two huge UK offshore wind projects win planning consent
item.description: Offshore wind farm being developed in two phases in North Sea by RWE and Masdar could power three million UK homes annually

item.title: Orsted hints at renewed appetite for growth in key APAC markets including South Korea and Australia
item.description: Leading renewables developer said measures taken to strengthen capital structure and financial foundations mean the company is ready to pursue new 'value-creating opportunities' within offshore wind

item.title: Mingyang boss reveals new plan to win orders in Europe
item.description: Leading offshore wind turbine supplier believes models designed in Europe will be ‘much easier’ to sell internationally

item.title: Offshore wind vs data centres: battle for capital is on, says key bank
item.description: European sector needs to broaden lender base and carefully structure financing to meet 'intense competition' from other infrastructure segments, says Japan's MUFG

item.title: Interior chief attacks permitting 'logjam' while defending Trump's wind and solar roadblocks
item.description: Interior secretary Doug Burgum defended administration's blocking of renewables while lamenting 'red tape' that is holding back $1.5trn in energy investment

item.title: Enercon to open Turkey blade factory to fulfil local content rules
item.description: Factory in Izmir province to churn out blades mostly for local market but also for exports to Southern and Eastern Europe

item.title: King Charles unveils new UK legislation to bolster energy security
item.description: Long-awaited bill sets out the government's plans to transition away from fossil fuels and bolster energy security, including through new wind farms and solar capacity

item.title: Battery surge fails to offset solar cannibalisation: lessons for wind
item.description: Wind and energy storage must heed lessons from China’s ballooning solar sector

item.title: Trailblazing foreign wind giant tipped for China offshore exit
item.description: French group EDF said to be looking at sale of renewable energy assets seven years after taking stake in Chinese projects

item.title: First power flows from giant RWE offshore projects as better winds swell profits
item.description: German energy major also posts one-off gain from Dutch compensation payment

item.title: Wind farm death after crane falls from cliff
item.description: Construction worker dies after crane topples from road at site of renewable energy project in South Korea

item.title: Europe's two turbine maker reality for offshore wind – and how to make it work
item.description: Meeting Europe’s fixed-bottom offshore wind needs with just Vestas and Siemens Gamesa as turbine suppliers is tough, but possible – however floating is a different problem, warns John MacAskill of ABL Group

item.title: Clearway Energy's solar deal points to derisked renewables push
item.description: Clearway Energy's acquisition of Deriva's solar-plus-storage portfolio reflects wider shift towards operating assets to meet hyperscaler demand as Trump risk threatens power capacity ramp

item.title: Mingyang gets go-ahead for colossal wind and green hydrogen project
item.description: The Chinese wind turbine giant had originally signed a $10bn investment agreement, but has now boosted its planned spending

item.title: Three best European markets to invest in co-located renewables and battery storage
item.description: Paring storage with renewables will be key to reduce costs and grid constraints, report points out

item.title: The scale of Big Oil's retreat from wind and solar – and why it's speeding up
item.description: Major players all cut clean energy spending even before impact of latest crisis tilts them further towards core segments, says BNEF

item.title: Siemens Energy CEO: Offshore wind projects not getting FIDs fast enough
item.description: All projects auctioned off at lower interest rates years ago now face challenges as rates have risen, Christian Bruch said

item.title: Siemens Gamesa 'on track to break even' as first 7MW orders trickle in
item.description: Parent Siemens Energy says wind turbine unit’s loss narrows driven by onshore news units business

item.title: Data centres could trigger new ‘Spain-style’ blackouts and force grids to curb wind: Report
item.description: A high concentration of data centres could create local voltage problems that develop into frequency disturbances across the wider power system, according to a new report from the European transmission system operators, Entso-E

item.title: Crown Estate unveils fresh £15m boost for offshore wind supply chains
item.description: The investment is part of a push to strengthen domestic clean energy manufacturing amid growing concerns of dependence on international supply

item.title: Chinese giant plans Canada entry with large wind-storage project
item.description: Canada continues its trajectory to add wind power as the US backs away

item.title: Chinese giant plans Canada entry with large wind-storage project
item.description: Canada continues its trajectory to add wind power as the US backs away

item.title: Iran oil crisis: geography of energy risk is changing with renewables, not disappearing
item.description: Despite the energy independence promised by batteries, the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint shows risks of overconcentrating green tech supply in one country

item.title: Enercon CEO expects only indirect impact from Iran war on business
item.description: German wind turbine maker has no business in affected countries and does not source materials from the region, so crisis not likely to have direct effect, CEO Udo Bauer tells Recharge

item.title: Suzlon eyes building new wind turbine factory in Europe
item.description: Indian wind turbine giant is going through a major strategy revamp as it pushes into new markets and technologies, including solar panels and battery storage
